title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Probe Connector Damage or Probe Not Recognized
description: Guide for troubleshooting probe recognition issues and possible connector damage on a Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the Edge II and unplug AC power if connected. Wait 30–60 seconds, then restart the system. Reason: Resets internal communication between the system and probe.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Connector and Cable
steps.instructions: Check for bent pins, debris, corrosion, or visible damage on the probe connector. Inspect the probe cable for cuts, fraying, or kinks. Reason: Physical damage can prevent proper connection and recognition.

steps.title: Clean the Connector Contacts
steps.instructions: Use a lint-free cloth or approved isopropyl alcohol wipe on the probe connector and system port. Ensure the system is powered off during cleaning. Reason: Dirt or residue can block proper electrical contact.

steps.title: Reconnect the Probe Firmly
steps.instructions: Insert the probe fully into the system port until it clicks or seats properly. Avoid twisting or forcing the connector. Reason: Loose connections are a common cause of 'not detected' errors.

steps.title: Test with Another Compatible Probe
steps.instructions: If available, connect a different, known-working probe to the same port. If the new probe is recognized, the original probe is likely faulty. Reason: Isolates whether the issue is the probe or the system connector.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Probe on Another Edge II Unit
steps.instructions: If another Edge II is available, connect the probe there. If it fails on multiple systems, the probe itself is likely defective.

CCR.Complaint: Probe not detected on the Edge II.
CCR.Cause: Probe connector showed bent pins and intermittent contact; system did not recognize probe.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned connector, tested with another probe, confirmed original probe faulty; labeled probe Out of Service and sent for repair.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Image Artifacts or Degraded Image Quality
description: Guide to troubleshoot image artifacts or degraded quality on Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound systems, focusing on external causes first.

steps.title: Verify Probe Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the probe is fully seated in the connector. Loose or partially connected probes can cause artifacts. Inspect the connector for bent pins or debris.

steps.title: Swap or Inspect the Probe
steps.instructions: Test with a known-good probe to see if the artifact persists. If the image improves, the original probe may be damaged.

steps.title: Check the Cable and Strain Relief
steps.instructions: Examine the probe cable for kinks, cuts, or frayed areas. Damaged cables can cause intermittent signal loss.

steps.title: Review System Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm presets and imaging modes are correct for the intended study. Reset the image settings to default to rule out incorrect gain, depth, or frequency settings.

steps.title: Check the Display and Monitor Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect video output cables and monitor connections for loose connections or damage. Confirm the display itself is functioning correctly by swapping monitors if available.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn the system off, wait 30–60 seconds, then restart. This can resolve temporary software glitches affecting image processing.

steps.title: Run Built-in Self-Test / Diagnostic Tools
steps.instructions: Utilize the Edge II’s internal diagnostics to check for probe or system errors. Document any error codes for escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound images appear grainy and show streaking artifacts during exams.
CCR.Cause: Probe was properly seated but the same artifact persisted on multiple probes; system presets verified; cables and display connections intact.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Battery Charging Issues
description: Troubleshooting guide for battery charging issues on the Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system, focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the system is plugged into a known working outlet. Test the outlet with another device or a voltmeter. Reason: A faulty outlet or power strip is a common cause of battery not charging.

steps.title: Check Power Cord and Adapter
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord for cuts, frays, or bent connectors. Confirm the AC adapter matches the specifications for the Edge II. Swap with a known-good cord/adapter if available. Reason: Damaged cords or incorrect adapters prevent proper charging.

steps.title: Confirm Battery Seating
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the battery in the device. Ensure the battery clicks fully into place and terminals align correctly. Reason: Poor contact can prevent charging or cause intermittent detection.

steps.title: Inspect Battery for Damage
steps.instructions: Look for swelling, leaks, corrosion, or unusual heat. Reason: Physical damage indicates the battery should be removed from service.

steps.title: Observe System Indicators
steps.instructions: Check the on-screen battery icon or LEDs. Note any error codes or messages displayed when connected to AC. Reason: System diagnostics may identify whether the issue is the battery or device circuitry.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Battery
steps.instructions: If available, insert a different, fully charged Edge II battery. Observe if it charges normally. Reason: Confirms whether the issue is with the battery or the system.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Shut down the ultrasound, remove AC power and battery, wait 1–2 minutes, then reconnect and power on. Reason: Resets charging circuitry and clears minor firmware glitches.

CCR.Complaint: Battery not charging, system only works on AC power.
CCR.Cause: Observed that the AC outlet and adapter are functioning, battery seated correctly, but the system does not charge. Battery indicator remains static.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Battery inspected: Yes, no visible damage
Error messages observed: None or specific battery alert
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Buttons or Keypad Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot unresponsive or malfunctioning buttons and keypad on Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system.

steps.title: Confirm System Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the ultrasound system is powered on and the battery has sufficient charge or the unit is connected to AC power. Why: A low battery or unstable power can cause input lag or unresponsive keys.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Keypad
steps.instructions: Check for visible debris, liquid contamination, or damage around keys. Why: Dirt, dust, or spills can prevent proper key contact.

steps.title: Clean the Keypad
steps.instructions: Use a lint-free cloth lightly dampened with isopropyl alcohol to clean around and on the keys. Avoid excessive liquid. Why: Cleaning removes dirt and oils that may block electrical contact.

steps.title: Check for External Key Lock or Function Mode
steps.instructions: Verify that any “lock” or “function mode” settings on the system are not engaged (refer to system touchscreen menus). Why: Some ultrasound systems allow locking buttons to prevent accidental presses.

steps.title: Test All Keys Methodically
steps.instructions: Press each button or key individually while observing system response. Note any keys that are non-responsive or inconsistent. Why: Pinpoints which buttons are failing and helps rule out software vs. hardware issues.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled system reboot. Why: Sometimes software glitches can temporarily disable keypad input.

steps.title: Check for Firmware Updates
steps.instructions: Ensure the device firmware is current per manufacturer guidance. Why: Firmware updates can resolve known input or keypad issues.

CCR.Complaint: Several keys on the Edge II keypad do not respond, and imaging cannot be fully controlled.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed no liquid damage. Keypad cleaning and system restart did not restore functionality. Likely internal keypad failure.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for vendor repair.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - System Boot or Software Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting boot failures, startup errors, and software crashes on the Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the system is plugged into a known working outlet. Check power cords and connections for damage or looseness. Confirm that the AC power indicator is on. Why it matters: Many “boot failures” are actually due to unstable or missing power.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition
steps.instructions: If running on battery, connect AC power and observe LED indicators. Swap with a known good battery if available. Why it matters: Weak or depleted batteries can prevent proper boot.

steps.title: Inspect External Cables and Peripherals
steps.instructions: Disconnect all probes, USB devices, and network cables. Attempt boot with minimal configuration. Why it matters: Faulty peripherals can halt software initialization.

steps.title: Soft Reset / Safe Boot (if available)
steps.instructions: Follow manufacturer’s procedure to perform a soft reset or safe boot mode. Observe any error messages displayed. Why it matters: Safe boot may allow the system to bypass minor software issues.

steps.title: Check for Visible Damage or Overheating
steps.instructions: Inspect for unusual heat, burning smell, or liquid damage. Ensure vents are clear and fan is functional. Why it matters: Overheating or physical damage can prevent proper startup.

steps.title: Observe Error Codes
steps.instructions: Document any messages or codes that appear on startup. Refer to manufacturer support resources for error code meanings.

CCR.Complaint: Edge II will not boot and shows ‘Software Error 101’ on startup.
CCR.Cause: Power confirmed stable, battery verified, no external peripherals attached, soft reset failed; system still displays error.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to the repair bench for evaluation.
